paper_title: Photocatalytic CO2 methanation over NiO/In2O3 promoted TiO2 nanocatalysts using H2O and/or H2 reductants

catalyst.source: Photocatalytic CO2 methanation over NiO/In2O3 promoted TiO2 nanocatalysts using H2O and/or H2 reductants
catalyst.context: Photocatalytic CO2 methanation over NiO/In2O3 promoted TiO2 nanocatalysts using H2O and/or H2 reductants

catalyst.llm generation: NiO and In2O3 promoted TiO2 nanocatalysts
catalyst.source: The NiO and In 2 O 3 promoted TiO 2 nanocatalysts were prepared and tested for the photocatalytic CO 2 reduction using H 2 O and/H 2 reductant.
catalyst.context: The NiO and In 2 O 3 promoted TiO 2 nanocatalysts were prepared and tested for the photocatalytic CO 2 reduction using H 2 O and/H 2 reductant.The deeper insight on the reactivity and selectivity was obtained using different metals and reductant.The prepared NiO and In 2 O 3 based TiO 2 photocatalysts showed enhanced photocatalytic CO 2 reduction.The formation of the nanostructure was favorable for the separation of the photo-generated charges.The porous structure of NiO-In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 effectively provides reacting sites for transforming electrons to the reactants and prevents electron-hole recombination.Using H 2 O reductant, the photoactivity of NiO-In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 catalyst was the highest for CH 4 production, which exceeded 2 fold than In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 and 6.5 times than the pure TiO 2 .The enhanced photoactivity was evidently due to hindered charges recombination rate and their efficient transfer toward Ni and In metal.On the other hand, with H 2 reductant, CO was detected as the major product with appreciable amount of CH 4 .The total amount of products (CO, CH 4 ) over the NiO-In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 catalyst using H 2 reductant was 1.5 times more than using H 2 O under the same operating conditions.The fundamentals derived from these experiments are useful in the design of co-catalysts and selection of reductant for production of selective fuels. Fig. 1.Schematic of experimental set-up for photocatalytic CO 2 reduction with H 2 O and/or H 2 . Fig. 3. FESEM micrographs of NiO and In 2 O 3 doped TiO 2 samples: (a and b) SEM and mapping images of TiO 2 , (c and d) SEM and mapping image of 3 In 2 O 3 /TiO 3 , (e and f) SEM and mapping images of 1 NiO-3In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 samples. Fig. 7. (a) N 2 adsorption-desorption isotherms of TiO 2 , 3 In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 and 1 NiO-3 In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 samples; and (b) pore size distribution of corresponding samples. b) shows production of CO during CO 2 reduction by H 2 O versus irradiation time over the NiO-loaded In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 samples. Fig. 10.Effect of NiO-promoted In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 catalysts for CH 4 , CO and C 2 H 6 production of photocatalytic CO 2 reduction with H 2 O vapors at various Ni-loading and irradiation times under UV-light irradiations: (a) CH 4 evolution, (b) CO evolution and (c) C 2 H 6 production. Summary of physical-chemical characteristics of TiO 2 samples. (a).Pure TiO 2 has smaller CO 2 reduction efficiency, but is Fig. 6.XPS spectra of 3 In 3 O 3 /TiO 2 and 1 NiO-3 In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 samples: (a) Ti 2p spectra, (b) O 1s spectra, (c) C1s spectra, (d) spectra of In 3d, and (e) spectrum of Ni 2p.considerably enhanced in the TiO 2 samples loaded with In.Inloaded TiO 2 samples has effectively promoted CO 2 methanation with an optimum of 3 In-contents.Therefore, 3 In-loaded TiO 2 is the most effective catalyst over which amount of evolved CH 4 reached to 249 lmole g A1 after 2 h of irradiation time, significantly higher than the pure TiO 2 .This enhancement in TiO 2 photoactivity of CH 4 production during CO 2 reduction was due to In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 mesoporous structure and hindered charges recombination rate by In-metal.At excess amount of In-loading, lower CH 4 yield can be attributed to the formation of charge recombination centers over the TiO 2 surface Summary of products produced during photocatalytic CO 2 reduction with H 2 O and/or H 2 reductants using TiO 2 and metal-loaded TiO 2 catalysts.Selectivity of product C i 14 moles of Ci in product Total moles of C produced A 100: Fig. 13.Proposed schematic of charge transfer and reactants transformation to CO and CH 4 over NiO and In 2 O 3 modified TiO 2 photocatalyst using H 2 O and/or H 2 reductant under UV-light irradiation. : b The authors would like to extend their deepest appreciation to Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM), Malaysia for financial support of this research under PAS RUG (Potential Academic Staff Research University Grant).

catalyst.llm generation: NiO and In2O3 based TiO2 photocatalysts
catalyst.source: The prepared NiO and In 2 O 3 based TiO 2 photocatalysts showed enhanced photocatalytic CO 2 reduction.
catalyst.context: The NiO and In 2 O 3 promoted TiO 2 nanocatalysts were prepared and tested for the photocatalytic CO 2 reduction using H 2 O and/H 2 reductant.The deeper insight on the reactivity and selectivity was obtained using different metals and reductant.The prepared NiO and In 2 O 3 based TiO 2 photocatalysts showed enhanced photocatalytic CO 2 reduction.The formation of the nanostructure was favorable for the separation of the photo-generated charges.The porous structure of NiO-In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 effectively provides reacting sites for transforming electrons to the reactants and prevents electron-hole recombination.Using H 2 O reductant, the photoactivity of NiO-In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 catalyst was the highest for CH 4 production, which exceeded 2 fold than In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 and 6.5 times than the pure TiO 2 .The enhanced photoactivity was evidently due to hindered charges recombination rate and their efficient transfer toward Ni and In metal.On the other hand, with H 2 reductant, CO was detected as the major product with appreciable amount of CH 4 .The total amount of products (CO, CH 4 ) over the NiO-In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 catalyst using H 2 reductant was 1.5 times more than using H 2 O under the same operating conditions.The fundamentals derived from these experiments are useful in the design of co-catalysts and selection of reductant for production of selective fuels. Fig. 1.Schematic of experimental set-up for photocatalytic CO 2 reduction with H 2 O and/or H 2 . Fig. 3. FESEM micrographs of NiO and In 2 O 3 doped TiO 2 samples: (a and b) SEM and mapping images of TiO 2 , (c and d) SEM and mapping image of 3 In 2 O 3 /TiO 3 , (e and f) SEM and mapping images of 1 NiO-3In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 samples. Fig. 7. (a) N 2 adsorption-desorption isotherms of TiO 2 , 3 In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 and 1 NiO-3 In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 samples; and (b) pore size distribution of corresponding samples. b) shows production of CO during CO 2 reduction by H 2 O versus irradiation time over the NiO-loaded In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 samples. Fig. 10.Effect of NiO-promoted In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 catalysts for CH 4 , CO and C 2 H 6 production of photocatalytic CO 2 reduction with H 2 O vapors at various Ni-loading and irradiation times under UV-light irradiations: (a) CH 4 evolution, (b) CO evolution and (c) C 2 H 6 production. Summary of physical-chemical characteristics of TiO 2 samples. (a).Pure TiO 2 has smaller CO 2 reduction efficiency, but is Fig. 6.XPS spectra of 3 In 3 O 3 /TiO 2 and 1 NiO-3 In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 samples: (a) Ti 2p spectra, (b) O 1s spectra, (c) C1s spectra, (d) spectra of In 3d, and (e) spectrum of Ni 2p.considerably enhanced in the TiO 2 samples loaded with In.Inloaded TiO 2 samples has effectively promoted CO 2 methanation with an optimum of 3 In-contents.Therefore, 3 In-loaded TiO 2 is the most effective catalyst over which amount of evolved CH 4 reached to 249 lmole g A1 after 2 h of irradiation time, significantly higher than the pure TiO 2 .This enhancement in TiO 2 photoactivity of CH 4 production during CO 2 reduction was due to In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 mesoporous structure and hindered charges recombination rate by In-metal.At excess amount of In-loading, lower CH 4 yield can be attributed to the formation of charge recombination centers over the TiO 2 surface Summary of products produced during photocatalytic CO 2 reduction with H 2 O and/or H 2 reductants using TiO 2 and metal-loaded TiO 2 catalysts.Selectivity of product C i 14 moles of Ci in product Total moles of C produced A 100: Fig. 13.Proposed schematic of charge transfer and reactants transformation to CO and CH 4 over NiO and In 2 O 3 modified TiO 2 photocatalyst using H 2 O and/or H 2 reductant under UV-light irradiation. : b The authors would like to extend their deepest appreciation to Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM), Malaysia for financial support of this research under PAS RUG (Potential Academic Staff Research University Grant).

catalyst.source: Using H 2 O reductant, the photoactivity of NiO-In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 catalyst was the highest for CH 4 production, which exceeded 2 fold than In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 and 6.5 times than the pure TiO 2 .

FESEM micrographs of NiO and In 2 O 3 doped TiO 2 samples: (a and b) SEM and mapping images of TiO 2 , (c and d) SEM and mapping image of 3 In 2 O 3 /TiO 3 , (e and f) SEM and mapping images of 1 NiO-3In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 samples. Fig. 7. (a) N 2 adsorption-desorption isotherms of TiO 2 , 3 In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 and 1 NiO-3 In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 samples; and (b) pore size distribution of corresponding samples. b) shows production of CO during CO 2 reduction by H 2 O versus irradiation time over the NiO-loaded In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 samples. Fig. 10.Effect of NiO-promoted In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 catalysts for CH 4 , CO and C 2 H 6 production of photocatalytic CO 2 reduction with H 2 O vapors at various Ni-loading and irradiation times under UV-light irradiations: (a) CH 4 evolution, (b) CO evolution and (c) C 2 H 6 production. Summary of physical-chemical characteristics of TiO 2 samples. (a).Pure TiO 2 has smaller CO 2 reduction efficiency, but is Fig. 6.XPS spectra of 3 In 3 O 3 /TiO 2 and 1 NiO-3 In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 samples: (a) Ti 2p spectra, (b) O 1s spectra, (c) C1s spectra, (d) spectra of In 3d, and (e) spectrum of Ni 2p.considerably enhanced in the TiO 2 samples loaded with In.Inloaded TiO 2 samples has effectively promoted CO 2 methanation with an optimum of 3 In-contents.Therefore, 3 In-loaded TiO 2 is the most effective catalyst over which amount of evolved CH 4 reached to 249 lmole g A1 after 2 h of irradiation time, significantly higher than the pure TiO 2 .This enhancement in TiO 2 photoactivity of CH 4 production during CO 2 reduction was due to In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 mesoporous structure and hindered charges recombination rate by In-metal.At excess amount of In-loading, lower CH 4 yield can be attributed to the formation of charge recombination centers over the TiO 2 surface Summary of products produced during photocatalytic CO 2 reduction with H 2 O and/or H 2 reductants using TiO 2 and metal-loaded TiO 2 catalysts.Selectivity of product C i 14 moles of Ci in product Total moles of C produced A 100: Fig. 13.Proposed schematic of charge transfer and reactants transformation to CO and CH 4 over NiO and In 2 O 3 modified TiO 2 photocatalyst using H 2 O and/or H 2 reductant under UV-light irradiation. : b The authors would like to extend their deepest appreciation to Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM), Malaysia for financial support of this research under PAS RUG (Potential Academic Staff Research University Grant).

Fig. 10.Effect of NiO-promoted In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 catalysts for CH 4 , CO and C 2 H 6 production of photocatalytic CO 2 reduction with H 2 O vapors at various Ni-loading and irradiation times under UV-light irradiations: (a) CH 4 evolution, (b) CO evolution and (c) C 2 H 6 production. (a).Pure TiO 2 has smaller CO 2 reduction efficiency, but is Fig. 6.XPS spectra of 3 In 3 O 3 /TiO 2 and 1 NiO-3 In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 samples: (a) Ti 2p spectra, (b) O 1s spectra, (c) C1s spectra, (d) spectra of In 3d, and (e) spectrum of Ni 2p.considerably enhanced in the TiO 2 samples loaded with In.Inloaded TiO 2 samples has effectively promoted CO 2 methanation with an optimum of 3 In-contents.Therefore, 3 In-loaded TiO 2 is the most effective catalyst over which amount of evolved CH 4 reached to 249 lmole g A1 after 2 h of irradiation time, significantly higher than the pure TiO 2 .This enhancement in TiO 2 photoactivity of CH 4 production during CO 2 reduction was due to In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 mesoporous structure and hindered charges recombination rate by In-metal.At excess amount of In-loading, lower CH 4 yield can be attributed to the formation of charge recombination centers over the TiO 2 surface Summary of products produced during photocatalytic CO 2 reduction with H 2 O and/or H 2 reductants using TiO 2 and metal-loaded TiO 2 catalysts.Selectivity of product C i 14 moles of Ci in product Total moles of C produced A 100: Fig. 13.Proposed schematic of charge transfer and reactants transformation to CO and CH 4 over NiO and In 2 O 3 modified TiO 2 photocatalyst using H 2 O and/or H 2 reductant under UV-light irradiation. : b The authors would like to extend their deepest appreciation to Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM), Malaysia for financial support of this research under PAS RUG (Potential Academic Staff Research University Grant).

light source.source: Fig. 10.Effect of NiO-promoted In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 catalysts for CH 4 , CO and C 2 H 6 production of photocatalytic CO 2 reduction with H 2 O vapors at various Ni-loading and irradiation times under UV-light irradiations: (a) CH 4 evolution, (b) CO evolution and (c) C 2 H 6 production.
light source.context: The NiO and In 2 O 3 promoted TiO 2 nanocatalysts were prepared and tested for the photocatalytic CO 2 reduction using H 2 O and/H 2 reductant.The deeper insight on the reactivity and selectivity was obtained using different metals and reductant.The prepared NiO and In 2 O 3 based TiO 2 photocatalysts showed enhanced photocatalytic CO 2 reduction.The formation of the nanostructure was favorable for the separation of the photo-generated charges.The porous structure of NiO-In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 effectively provides reacting sites for transforming electrons to the reactants and prevents electron-hole recombination.Using H 2 O reductant, the photoactivity of NiO-In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 catalyst was the highest for CH 4 production, which exceeded 2 fold than In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 and 6.5 times than the pure TiO 2 .The enhanced photoactivity was evidently due to hindered charges recombination rate and their efficient transfer toward Ni and In metal.On the other hand, with H 2 reductant, CO was detected as the major product with appreciable amount of CH 4 .The total amount of products (CO, CH 4 ) over the NiO-In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 catalyst using H 2 reductant was 1.5 times more than using H 2 O under the same operating conditions.The fundamentals derived from these experiments are useful in the design of co-catalysts and selection of reductant for production of selective fuels. Fig. 1.Schematic of experimental set-up for photocatalytic CO 2 reduction with H 2 O and/or H 2 . Fig. 3. Fig. 7. (a) N 2 adsorption-desorption isotherms of TiO 2 , 3 In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 and 1 NiO-3 In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 samples; and (b) pore size distribution of corresponding samples. b) shows production of CO during CO 2 reduction by H 2 O versus irradiation time over the NiO-loaded In 2 O 3 /TiO 2 samples.
